free_text: Water-cooled targets made by exposure of 0.025-cm gold
backings to vapor released by the reduction of CaO in
a tantalum boat.  For most targets, the initial
material was CaCo3 enriched to 99.97% in 40Ca.
Targets of various thicknesses, in the range from 2 to
40 keV for 4.5-MeV alpha particles, were used.
Coaxial 30 cm**3 Ge(Li) detector used to measure
NaI(Tl) detector used to monitor yield.
Data scanned from Figs. 7, 9, 11, 13 at NPDC
12.7*12.7 cm NaI(Tl) crystal used to measure
yield of gamma rays from the reaction.
Coaxial Ge(Li) detector of about the same volume as tha
used in 1st experiment used as monitor.
Data taken from Table II in PR/C 15 reference.
